tolerance.description: Euclidean cluster tolerance [m]. Two occupied voxels are merged into the same cluster when their 2D (x-y) centroid distance is within this value (applied transitively). Clustering is performed on voxel centroids with z flattened to 0.

voxel_leaf_size.description: Voxel leaf size in x and y [m]. The z leaf is effectively infinite, so the grid is 2D and all heights collapse into a single x-y cell. This value bounds the footprint resolution used for clustering and splitting.

min_points_number_per_voxel.description: Minimum number of raw points a voxel must contain to be kept. Voxels with fewer points are discarded before clustering, rejecting sparse noise.

min_cluster_size.description: Minimum number of raw points a cluster must contain to be included in the output. Smaller clusters are dropped at the final filtering stage.

max_cluster_size.description: Maximum cluster size in number of voxels, passed to the PCL Euclidean cluster extraction as its upper bound. Voxel groups larger than this are not formed as a single cluster.

max_voxel_cluster_for_output.description: Voxel-count threshold above which a cluster is treated as too large to be a valid object (e.g. a wall or building) and is dropped entirely from the output.

min_voxel_cluster_size_for_filtering.description: Voxel-count threshold above which a cluster is considered 'large' and subject to per-voxel point capping (see max_points_per_voxel_in_large_cluster). Clusters at or below this size keep all their points.

max_points_per_voxel_in_large_cluster.description: Maximum number of points kept per voxel within a large cluster (one whose voxel count exceeds min_voxel_cluster_size_for_filtering). Excess points are dropped to bound downstream cost; points are processed in a deterministically shuffled order so the kept points sample uniformly across each voxel.

use_height.description: Use point.z for clustering. Currently only the 2D (false) path is implemented.
